chapterTitle: Chapter 1: React Native, Expo & Your First Mobile App

MCQs.question: What is React Native primarily used for?

options.text: Building native mobile UIs with JavaScript/TypeScript and React
Writing Swift and Kotlin without React

MCQs.question: In React Native, raw text inside a View must be wrapped in:
Which command typically starts the Expo development server?
Expo Go is best described as:

options.text: A cloud database for Expo apps
An app on your phone that runs compatible Expo projects for quick testing
The only way to publish to the App Store
HTML elements in the browser only

MCQs.question: package.json in an Expo project mainly lists:

options.text: Expo app metadata: name, icons, splash, plugins

MCQs.question: Which is required to run the iOS Simulator on a Mac?

options.text: The JavaScript bundler used by React Native / Expo

MCQs.question: A development build (vs Expo Go) is needed when:

options.text: You need custom native code or modules not in Expo Go

ShortQuestions.question: In one paragraph, explain how React Native differs from a mobile website in a WebView.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: React Native renders **native UI components** driven by JavaScript, not an embedded browser. A WebView app displays **HTML/CSS** inside a browser component; performance, gestures, and access to native APIs differ. RN integrates with platform UI and navigation more like a native app.

ShortQuestions.question: List three files or folders you expect in a new Expo project and what each is for.
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: **package.json** — dependencies and scripts; **app.json** — Expo config (name, icons, splash); **App.tsx** or **app/** — application entry and UI/navigation depending on template.

ShortQuestions.question: Why must all user-visible strings be placed inside <Text>?
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: React Native does not use the DOM. **Text** maps to native text components; **View** is for layout. Raw strings in **View** are invalid because there is no HTML text node concept—platform text rendering goes through the **Text** component.

ShortQuestions.question: What is the role of Node.js in Expo development?
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: Node.js runs the **toolchain**: the Metro bundler, package managers, and CLI commands (`npx expo ...`). The app still runs on a device/simulator; Node powers **development and build** on your machine.

ShortQuestions.question: When would you use tunnel mode with expo start?
ShortQuestions.preferredAnswer: When the **phone and computer are not on the same LAN**, or **firewalls** block LAN access, **tunnel** routes traffic through Expo’s servers so the device can still load the bundle (often slower but more reliable on restrictive networks).
